task.title: Implement FE-17 - Bulk Review For LLM Suggestions
task.goal: Add or complete filter-based bulk approve and bulk reject operations for durable `LlmSuggestion` rows without redesigning the existing FE-15 single-review flow or the FE-16 feedback flow.

importantCurrentState: FE-15 already introduced durable `LlmSuggestion` rows, single-item review APIs, and materialization into real `TestCase` records.
FE-16 already introduced per-user `LlmSuggestionFeedback` and feedback-aware prompt refinement; FE-17 must stay additive to that work.

Current codebase or branch may already contain preliminary FE-17 files such as `BulkReviewLlmSuggestionsCommand`, `BulkReviewLlmSuggestionsRequest`, `BulkReviewLlmSuggestionsResultModel`, `ILlmSuggestionReviewService`, `LlmSuggestionReviewService`, and controller wiring. Inspect and reconcile before adding duplicate types.

agentRules: Per `AGENTS.md`, do not assume migrations or Docker wiring are fine. Verify the conclusion and report it explicitly.
If implementation unexpectedly changes EF model, DbContext, seed data, or migrations, create the migration only in `ClassifiedAds.Migrator` and run migration verification.
If implementation unexpectedly adds a host reference, runtime dependency, or Docker wiring change, inspect Dockerfiles and `docker-compose.yml` and run the required compose verification.

defaultImplementationChoices: Reuse durable `LlmSuggestion` rows created by FE-15; do not add a batch aggregate table.
Reuse `ILlmSuggestionReviewService` for materialization, audit, subscription checks, and suite-version updates instead of duplicating approval logic in the bulk command handler.
Support only `Approve` and `Reject` actions in FE-17 v1.
Select suggestions by `TestSuiteId` plus optional FE-15 style filters: `FilterBySuggestionType`, `FilterByTestType`, and `FilterByEndpointId`.
Keep batch execution deterministic by ordering matched suggestions by `DisplayOrder` before processing.
Return a summary result model instead of a per-item API roundtrip.
hardConstraints: MUST build on top of FE-15 durable suggestion review instead of redesigning review storage.
MUST keep FE-17 additive to FE-15 and FE-16. Do not break single-item review or feedback APIs.
MUST NOT introduce new durable bulk-review tables, new cache state, or a new module unless a real blocker forces it.
MUST NOT dispatch one single-review command per suggestion from the bulk endpoint. Load the matched rows once and process them as one bulk workflow.
MUST validate suite ownership using the same suite-owner rule already used by FE-15 and FE-16.
MUST reject unsupported actions. FE-17 v1 accepts only `Approve` and `Reject`.
MUST require `ReviewNotes` when bulk action is `Reject`.
MUST leave already-reviewed or superseded suggestions untouched by selecting only `Pending` rows.
MUST use ASCII in new files.

selectionRules: Apply optional filters for suggestion type, test type, and endpoint id.
Order matched suggestions by `DisplayOrder` before processing.
If no suggestions match, return `200 OK` with zero counts and no side effects.

approveFlow: Validate suite existence and suite ownership.
Load all matching pending suggestions in one query.

Reuse the same materialization semantics as FE-15: create `TestCase`, `TestCaseRequest`, `TestCaseExpectation`, `TestCaseVariable`, `TestCaseChangeLog`, and `TestSuiteVersion` records transactionally.
Increment `MaxTestCasesPerSuite` usage once for the whole batch.
Return summary fields including processed suggestion ids and applied test case ids.
rejectFlow: Validate suite existence and suite ownership.
Load all matching pending suggestions in one query.
Update suggestion review metadata without materializing any `TestCase` rows.

rules: `MatchedCount` and `ProcessedCount` should be identical in the v1 all-or-nothing flow.
`AppliedTestCaseIds` should be empty for bulk reject.
`MaterializedCount` should equal the number of created test cases for bulk approve.

leaveUnchangedUnlessBlocked: FE-15 single review endpoint and request/response contracts.
FE-16 feedback persistence and feedback-summary query wiring.
Host `Program.cs`, Dockerfiles, and `docker-compose.yml` unless a real new dependency is introduced.
outOfScope: Bulk modify-and-approve with per-item edited payloads.
Bulk feedback submission across many suggestions.
A background job or queue-based bulk processor.
A new module, host, cache backend, or runtime dependency.

mustCover: approve only processes matching pending suggestions
reject only processes matching pending suggestions
approve path materializes test cases and updates suite version once
reject path does not materialize test cases
approve path increments subscription usage once for the batch
result model contains processed suggestion ids and applied test case ids

conditionalCommands: If shared single-review logic changed, also run: dotnet test 'ClassifiedAds.UnitTests/ClassifiedAds.UnitTests.csproj' --no-restore --filter 'FullyQualifiedName~ReviewLlmSuggestionCommandHandlerTests'
If EF-related files changed unexpectedly, run: dotnet build 'ClassifiedAds.Migrator/ClassifiedAds.Migrator.csproj' --no-restore
If EF-related files changed unexpectedly, run: dotnet 'ClassifiedAds.Migrator/bin/Debug/net10.0/ClassifiedAds.Migrator.dll' --verify-migrations'
If Docker-related files changed, run: docker compose config
If `ClassifiedAds.Migrator/Dockerfile` changed, run: docker compose build migrator
If `ClassifiedAds.WebAPI/Dockerfile` changed, run: docker compose build webapi
If `ClassifiedAds.Background/Dockerfile` changed, run: docker compose build background
dockerRegistrationCheck: Inspect `ClassifiedAds.Migrator/Dockerfile`, `ClassifiedAds.WebAPI/Dockerfile`, `ClassifiedAds.Background/Dockerfile`, and `docker-compose.yml` even if no change is expected.
Report explicitly whether FE-17 required any Docker or compose update.

doneCriteria: A bulk review endpoint exists on the existing `LlmSuggestionsController` surface.
FE-17 supports filter-based bulk approve and bulk reject for pending suggestions only.
Bulk approve reuses shared materialization logic and preserves FE-15 audit and suite-version behavior.
Bulk reject updates review metadata without creating test cases.
No unnecessary EF schema, module, Docker, or compose changes were introduced.
Targeted tests were added or updated and executed.
The final implementation report states whether migration verification was needed, whether Docker registration was checked, and whether any step was skipped.
